finding: Participants attribute knowledge more generously to agents performing morally or normatively negative actions than positive actions in Gettier scenarios, despite identical epistemic conditions; action valence influences belief attribution even when traditional epistemic factors are held constant.

Electorate political preference dispersion correlates negatively with government size in democracies but not autocracies; disagreement about proper scope of state reduces public spending independent of wealth or median voter.

Recent crime victimization predicts increased political participation across continents and crime types; effect magnitude rivals education as predictor of engagement, reversing expectations that victimization reduces civic activity.

Societal prevalence of emancipative values elevates and amplifies individual-level effects on nonviolent protest; ecological effect of value climate explains more cross-national protest variance than individual values alone.

Police killing of unarmed Black American in United States negatively affects Black Londoners' attitudes toward local police; effect concentrates among Black residents, demonstrating transnational consequences of racialized state violence.

Cigarette smoking and cannabis use equally predict psychotic-like experience frequency; smoking alone predicts distress; association between moderate cannabis use and distress is confounded by smoking.
